message:
chore: update beads issue status tracking

Status changes:
- bd-1mbs (Ctrl+V image paste): open → in_progress
- bd-2sl9 (SSE parser proptest): open → in_progress

These beads are actively being worked on following proptest addition
and ongoing input handling improvements.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1jdk","title":"Workstream: Model Selector + Scoped Cycling Parity","description":"# Goal
Match legacy model-selection UX:
- `Ctrl+L` opens a model selector.
- `Ctrl+P` / `Shift+Ctrl+P` cycles models forward/back within a scope.
- `/scoped-models` configures the scope for cycling.

# Legacy Spec
- Legacy README: Keyboard Shortcuts and Commands tables.
- Legacy docs:
  - `legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/docs/settings.md` (`enabledModels` patterns for cycling)

# Current Rust State (Gap)
- Interactive has `/model` command but no selector UI.
- Interactive has `/scoped-models` but:
  - no Ctrl+P cycling behavior
  - scope is not persisted
  - current Ctrl+P/N are used for input history navigation

# Scope / Deliverables
1) Model selector UI
- Show available models (from `ModelRegistry`) with fuzzy search.
- Selecting a model updates agent provider/model + persists to session header.

2) Scoped cycling
- Define the cycling list:
  - if `enabled_models`/`--models` patterns provided → use filtered list
  - else cycle all available models
- Implement forward/backward cycle with stable ordering.

3) `/scoped-models`
- Provide an in-app UI to edit scoped models (enable/disable patterns or pick from list).
- Persist scoped model patterns to settings (`enabled_models`).

# Dependencies
- Keybindings workstream (`bd-3ip`) is required to map Ctrl+L/Ctrl+P consistently.
- Unit tests for model registry scope logic are already tracked in `bd-2yd`.

# Testing
- State tests for model selector open/select.
- Deterministic tests for scoped cycling ordering.

## Acceptance Criteria
[ ] Ctrl+L opens model selector
[ ] Ctrl+P cycles models forward; Shift+Ctrl+P cycles backward
[ ] /scoped-models configures cycling scope and persists
[ ] Cycling respects enabled_models patterns
","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Ctrl+L opens model selector
[ ] Ctrl+P cycles models forward; Shift+Ctrl+P cycles backward
[ ] /scoped-models configures cycling scope and persists
[ ] Cycling respects enabled_models patterns","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:45:39.940944943Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T20:00:26.417626551Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jdk","depends_on_id":"bd-2qk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jn","title":"Impl: Runtime hardening (limits, watchdog, accounting)","description":"# Goal
Harden the PiJS runtime so extension execution is **bounded, observable, and safe**.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Memory cap + allocation tracking for QuickJS runtime.
- CPU/time budget via interrupt hook or watchdog.
- Hostcall timeouts enforced centrally.
- Structured metrics: per-extension CPU, memory, hostcall counts.
- Explicit termination reasons with user-friendly messages.

# Security / Reliability Invariants
- Extension cannot exceed configured budgets.
- Violations terminate the extension in a controlled, logged way.

# Tests
- Unit tests for budget exceed -> deterministic error.
- E2E security suite validates enforcement + log output.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:22:22.240908810Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T02:54:03.841995039Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jn","depends_on_id":"bd-2ke","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1kza","title":"Interactive: /share uploads session HTML as private GitHub gist","description":"# Goal
Implement gist-backed `/share` parity with legacy.

# Required Behavior
1) Verify `gh` exists.
2) Verify `gh auth status` succeeds.
3) Export session to a temp HTML file.
4) Show a cancellable loader while running:
   - `gh gist create --public=false <tmpFile>`
5) On success:
   - parse the returned gist URL
   - extract gist ID
   - compute viewer URL (see `bd-2uji`)
   - show both viewer URL and gist URL
6) Cleanup temp file.

# Cancellation
- If user cancels while `gh` is running:
  - kill the process
  - restore the editor
  - show status “Share cancelled”

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Works end-to-end with real `gh`.
- [ ] Produces the correct viewer URL.
- [ ] Cancellation never leaves a broken UI state.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:43:13.532989668Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:43:40.863399794Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1kza","depends_on_id":"bd-1u0c","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kza","depends_on_id":"bd-2v6k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1mbs","title":"Editor: Ctrl+V image paste + drag/drop attachment","description":"# Goal
Support image attachment UX in interactive mode:
- Ctrl+V to paste an image from clipboard.
- Drag/drop a file onto the terminal to attach.

# Legacy Behavior
- On Ctrl+V, legacy tries to read clipboard image; if present, it writes to a temp file and inserts the file path at cursor.
  - See `legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/src/modes/interactive/interactive-mode.ts` (`handleClipboardImagePaste`).

# Required Behavior
- Ctrl+V:
  - If clipboard contains an image, write it to a temp file (png/jpg based on mime if possible).
  - Insert the resulting path into the editor at the cursor.
  - If clipboard has no image or access denied, do nothing silently.

- Drag/drop:
  - Many terminals paste a quoted or escaped file path. Ensure we normalize/sanitize so that subsequent file reference processing can resolve it.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Users can attach an image without manually saving a file.
- [ ] No crashes when clipboard access is unavailable.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:40:05.385818509Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:40:42.357212214Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1mbs","depends_on_id":"bd-1iwi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1mbs","depends_on_id":"bd-gze","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}
+{"id":"bd-1mbs","title":"Editor: Ctrl+V image paste + drag/drop attachment","description":"# Goal
Support image attachment UX in interactive mode:
- Ctrl+V to paste an image from clipboard.
- Drag/drop a file onto the terminal to attach.

# Legacy Behavior
- On Ctrl+V, legacy tries to read clipboard image; if present, it writes to a temp file and inserts the file path at cursor.
  - See `legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/src/modes/interactive/interactive-mode.ts` (`handleClipboardImagePaste`).

# Required Behavior
- Ctrl+V:
  - If clipboard contains an image, write it to a temp file (png/jpg based on mime if possible).
  - Insert the resulting path into the editor at the cursor.
  - If clipboard has no image or access denied, do nothing silently.

- Drag/drop:
  - Many terminals paste a quoted or escaped file path. Ensure we normalize/sanitize so that subsequent file reference processing can resolve it.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Users can attach an image without manually saving a file.
- [ ] No crashes when clipboard access is unavailable.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:40:05.385818509Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T03:18:44.115803308Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1mbs","depends_on_id":"bd-1iwi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1mbs","depends_on_id":"bd-gze","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1mh","title":"Update session picker to use SQLite index","description":"# Update session picker to use SQLite index

## Goal
Replace filesystem walking in session picker with fast SQLite index queries.

## Current Implementation (src/session_picker.rs)
Currently scans filesystem:
```rust
// Slow: walks all session directories
let sessions = walkdir::WalkDir::new(&sessions_dir)
    .into_iter()
    .filter_map(|e| e.ok())
    .filter(|e| e.path().extension() == Some("jsonl"))
    .collect();
```

## New Implementation
```rust
use crate::session_index::SessionIndex;

impl SessionPicker {
    pub fn load_sessions(cwd: Option<&str>) -> Result<Vec<SessionMeta>> {
        let index = SessionIndex::open_default()?;
        index.list_sessions(cwd)
    }
}
```

## UI Changes
- Display indexed metadata directly (no file reads needed)
- Show message count from index
- Show session name from index
- Faster render (no filesystem I/O)

## Fallback Behavior
If index unavailable or empty:
```rust
fn load_sessions_with_fallback(cwd: Option<&str>) -> Result<Vec<SessionMeta>> {
    // Try index first
    if let Ok(index) = SessionIndex::open_default() {
        if let Ok(sessions) = index.list_sessions(cwd) {
            if !sessions.is_empty() {
                return Ok(sessions);
            }
        }
    }
    
    // Fallback to filesystem (and populate index)
    let sessions = load_sessions_from_filesystem(cwd)?;
    // Optionally trigger reindex in background
    Ok(sessions)
}
```

## Dependencies
- bd-3nz (indexing must work first)

## Testing
- Test: Session picker shows indexed sessions
- Test: Picker falls back to filesystem if needed
- Test: Performance improved (measure time)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Picker queries index by default
- [ ] Fallback to filesystem works
- [ ] UI displays index metadata
- [ ] Noticeably faster for 50+ sessions","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:38:46.861448590Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:37:20.352770513Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T19:37:20.352674283Z","close_reason":"Session picker uses index with fallback","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1mh","depends_on_id":"bd-346","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1mh","depends_on_id":"bd-3nz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1mq","title":"Unit tests: CLI/main selection + system prompt building","description":"Goal:
- Add unit tests for core CLI/main.rs logic without mocks (real temp dirs + config fixtures) and with detailed logging.

Scope:
- Model selection + thinking resolution (select_model_and_thinking) across provider/model edge cases.
- System prompt assembly (build_system_prompt) including project context files and skills prompt.
- File argument handling (prepare_initial_message) for text + images.
- API key resolution fallbacks and error paths.

Logging Requirements:
- Use the shared TestHarness/TestLogger (bd-3ml) in each test.
- On failure, log inputs, resolved model selection, and prompt fragments.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Tests cover normal + error paths and do not use fake providers.
- Uses real filesystem temp dirs for context files.
- Logs include inputs, derived values, and assertion context.
- Deterministic, no network access.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T04:58:29.180313821Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T08:35:31.868787891Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T08:35:31.868656316Z","close_reason":"Completed: app module + CLI/system prompt tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1mq","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1mq","depends_on_id":"bd-3ml","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}

Deliverable: a coverage matrix + checklist committed as test-plan docs or issue comment.","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:18:38Z"},{"id":152,"issue_id":"bd-1nn","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Audit summary (2026-02-03):

 {"id":"bd-2rl","title":"Connector: Filesystem minimal API (capability-scoped)","description":"# Goal
Provide a **tight, minimal filesystem connector** that supplies just enough for extensions without Node/Bun.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Minimal API: read, write, list, stat, mkdir, delete (if required).
- Path allowlist + canonicalization; no path traversal.
- Symlink policy: either disallow or resolve to allowlist roots.
- Explicit capability scopes (read/write per path prefix).
- Structured logs for each FS operation (path hash + scope).

# Security Invariants
- Every FS call checks capability scope before side effects.
- All paths are normalized and validated.

# Tests
- Unit tests for allowlist enforcement + symlink escapes.
- E2E security suite attempts traversal with log verification.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FoggyHill","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:22:30.145210806Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:56:25.476115727Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T19:56:25.476050806Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2rl","depends_on_id":"bd-37z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2rl","depends_on_id":"bd-h04","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2skp","title":"Workstream: Message Queue Parity (steering vs follow-up)","description":"# Goal
Implement **legacy Pi message queue semantics** in interactive mode, including steering vs follow-up delivery, queue UI, and keybinding integration.

# Background (Legacy Spec)
Legacy pi-mono allows submitting messages while the agent is still working:

From `legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/README.md` → “Message Queue”:
- **Enter** queues a *steering* message, delivered after current tool execution (interrupts remaining tools)
- **Alt+Enter** queues a *follow-up* message, delivered only after the agent finishes all work
- **Escape** aborts and restores queued messages to editor
- **Alt+Up** retrieves queued messages back to editor

Settings (legacy `docs/settings.md`):
- `steeringMode` and `followUpMode` can be `"one-at-a-time"` (default) or `"all"`.

# Current Rust State (Gap)
- `src/config.rs` already has `steering_mode` / `follow_up_mode`, but interactive does not implement the queue.
- Interactive key handling is hard-coded; Enter submits only when idle.

# Scope / Deliverables
## 1) Queue data model
- Represent queued items as:
  - kind: steering | follow_up
  - text
  - timestamp/sequence
- Store in PiApp state; ensure deterministic ordering.

## 2) Delivery points (core semantics)
- **Steering**: deliver after the current tool finishes (or next safe checkpoint) and cancel remaining tool iterations for the current user request.
- **Follow-up**: deliver only once the agent reaches an “idle” boundary (no more tools, assistant finished).

## 3) Modes: one-at-a-time vs all
- `one-at-a-time`:
  - deliver at most one queued message per boundary, wait for response before sending next.
- `all`:
  - flush all queued messages at the boundary (preserving order), then process.

## 4) Keybinding behavior
- Enter while busy → enqueue steering
- Alt+Enter while busy → enqueue follow-up
- Escape while busy → abort + restore all queued to editor
- Alt+Up → move queued back into editor for editing

(These should be expressed as actions and wired via the keybindings workstream.)

## 5) UI
- Render queued messages in a dedicated area (legacy has a pending messages container).
- Clear, minimal indicators:
  - show count by type
  - show the actual queued text (truncated) so the user knows what will be sent

# Testing
- Deterministic state machine tests for:
  - enqueue ordering
  - delivery boundaries
  - one-at-a-time vs all
  - abort + restore

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Queue behavior matches legacy semantics.
- [ ] Modes are configurable via settings.
- [ ] UI makes queued messages visible and understandable.

# Dependencies
- Depends on keybinding actions (`bd-3ip`) for correct shortcut routing.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:37:36.354917446Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:37:36.361927478Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2skp","depends_on_id":"bd-2qk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2sl9","title":"Proptest: SSE parser robustness (chunking + boundaries)","description":"## Goal
Property-test the SSE parser so arbitrary chunk boundaries and adversarial inputs don’t break parsing invariants.

## Why
SSE parsing is extremely sensitive to chunk boundaries. Real networks can split at any byte.

## Invariants
- Parsing is chunk-boundary invariant: splitting the same byte stream into random chunks yields identical parsed events.
- Parser never panics.
- Parser either produces valid events or a well-typed error (no silent corruption).
- For known-valid SSE inputs, the parser produces the expected event sequence.

## Acceptance
- New proptest module exists for `src/sse.rs`.
- CI runtime stays bounded.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:05:06.300424890Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T22:05:06.309184171Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2sl9","depends_on_id":"bd-2lr3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}
+{"id":"bd-2sl9","title":"Proptest: SSE parser robustness (chunking + boundaries)","description":"## Goal
Property-test the SSE parser so arbitrary chunk boundaries and adversarial inputs don’t break parsing invariants.

## Why
SSE parsing is extremely sensitive to chunk boundaries. Real networks can split at any byte.

## Invariants
- Parsing is chunk-boundary invariant: splitting the same byte stream into random chunks yields identical parsed events.
- Parser never panics.
- Parser either produces valid events or a well-typed error (no silent corruption).
- For known-valid SSE inputs, the parser produces the expected event sequence.

## Acceptance
- New proptest module exists for `src/sse.rs`.
- CI runtime stays bounded.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:05:06.300424890Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T03:16:34.443476664Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2sl9","depends_on_id":"bd-2lr3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2sr","title":"Impl: node:child_process subset (spawn/kill + streams) for full sample","description":"# Goal
Implement the **minimum `node:child_process` subset** required for **all 16 extensions in `docs/extension-sample.json`** to run **unmodified** under the PiJS JS tier.

This bead exists because `child_process` semantics (spawn + events/streams) are the hardest Node surface area in the sample set.

# Genericity constraint (non-negotiable)
- **No per-extension exceptions**: implementation must not branch on extension id/name.
- The shim must be a generic `pi:node/child_process` module whose behavior depends only on inputs + policy/capabilities.
- If a sample exposes a semantic gap, fix it by tightening the generic contract + tests, not by adding special cases.

# Scope (must match pinned-sample usage)
## API
- `spawn(command, args?, options?)` returning a `ChildProcess`-like object.

## Options to accept (best-effort)
Pinned sample uses:
- `cwd`
- `detached: true|false`
- `shell: false` (explicit)
- `stdio: ['ignore'|'pipe', 'pipe', 'pipe']`

The shim must accept these options and behave compatibly. Unsupported options must fail fast with an actionable error (not silent ignore).

## ChildProcess object surface
Pinned sample requires:
- `.pid` (number)
- `.killed` (boolean)
- `.kill(signal?)`
- `.on('close', (code) => ...)`
- `.on('error', (err) => ...)`
- `.stdout.on('data', (chunk) => ...)`
- `.stderr.on('data', (chunk) => ...)`

### Stream chunk semantics
- `chunk` must support `toString()` and roundtrip the emitted bytes.

## Non-goals
- Full Node streams implementation
- `fork`/`execFile`/`execSync`, IPC channels

# Design constraints
- **Capability gated**: all process execution must go through the connector dispatcher (bd-h04) with `exec` capability.
- **Process-tree cleanup**: integrate with Pi’s existing process-tree cleanup semantics.
- **Backpressure + size limits**: prevent unbounded memory from stdout/stderr capture.
- **Deterministic test mode**: allow harness to run with mocked process execution / recorded outputs.

# Related compatibility
- `process.kill(pid, signal?)` is part of the Node globals shim (bd-3d0). For safety, it must be restricted to pids created by this `spawn` implementation or denied by policy.

# Compatibility strategy (must support both paths)
- Path A (preferred): provide a real shim that satisfies the Node API surface the bundled deps call.
- Path B (compiler assist): extc rewrite can optionally map `spawn(...)` patterns to `await pi.exec(...)` when semantics are provably preserved (generic pattern-based rewrite only).

# Acceptance (hard)
- Sample extensions that transitively depend on `child_process` run **without manual source edits**.
- Policy denial produces an actionable error and a structured audit log entry.
- No extension-id special-casing (enforced by review/tests).

# Tests (required)
- Unit tests: spawn lifecycle, stdout/stderr data delivery, events ordering, kill semantics, `.killed` behavior.
- Negative tests: denied `exec` capability.

 {"id":"bd-2tf","title":"Unit tests: extension discovery + install resolution","description":"Background:
- Discovery/install is user-facing and failure-prone; tests prevent regressions.

Steps:
- Unit tests for package source resolution (global vs project precedence).
- Tests for CLI flags (--extension/--no-extensions) and conflict handling.
- Validate diagnostics for missing/invalid extensions (actionable errors).
- Cover path normalization, symlink handling, and manifest discovery.

Logging requirements:
- Tests assert diagnostics include source, resolution path, and reason codes.

Acceptance:
- All discovery/install branches have unit coverage with deterministic outputs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:04:58.361767846Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T03:05:37.400281159Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2tf","depends_on_id":"bd-1e0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2tf","depends_on_id":"bd-3so","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}
